text.title: Team Development Activities
text.summary: Structured activities for trust building, skill development, and process improvement with evidence-backed practices and agent integration patterns.

list.item: Regular 1-on-1s with explicit agenda (block 30 min weekly per direct report)
Psychological safety — teams must be able to raise concerns without punishment
Transparent communication — share context behind decisions, not just decisions
Team-building activities tailored to team size and remote/in-person split

rule.statement: Keep the team charter short (one screen) and treat it as a living document; review every 8-10 weeks or after any team-composition change.
rule.rationale: Long charters are not read; short ones are consulted at conflicts. Staleness destroys trust in the document and by extension in the agreements it captures.

list.item: Skills matrix: rate each skill 1 (beginner) to 4 (expert); pair every "1" cell with a named owner and a quarterly improvement target
Pair programming and cross-training for knowledge transfer across sole-owner skills
Mentoring programs — internal, with explicit goals and check-in cadence
Training opportunities — budget per-person per quarter, not per-year

rule.statement: Skills-matrix scores must be evidence-backed (PRs, incidents, code reviews); self-reported scores without evidence drift toward inflation.
rule.rationale: Inflated matrices mask critical knowledge gaps that surface at the worst moment — when the sole expert is unavailable during an incident.

list.item: Retrospectives: every sprint; action items must have owner + due date + verification method in next retro
Process experiments: time-boxed (one sprint), measured, documented as ADR if adopted
Tool optimization: quarterly review; only add tooling that removes friction, not adds ceremony
Workload balancing: use contribution data (git shortlog, PR review counts) to surface imbalances

rule.statement: Retro action items without an owner and verification method are not action items — they are wishes.
rule.rationale: Unowned actions do not get done; unverified actions get closed as done regardless of outcome; both undermine retro trust over time.

antipattern.description: Skipping team building because "there's no time."
antipattern.reason: Dysfunction deferred costs more time than investment avoided; unresolved Storming can freeze a team for months.

antipattern.description: One-size-fits-all management style across all team members.
antipattern.reason: Different Tuckman stages and different experience levels require different leadership approaches; uniform style over-manages high performers and under-supports strugglers.
Individual blame destroys psychological safety and repeats the failure; root cause is almost always process, tooling, or system design.

text.reference: Team charter with mission, members, working agreements, and meeting cadence
Skills matrix template with 1-4 scale and gap-action column
Retrospective template with owner and due fields on all action items
